title: Conjugate of a Complex Number
summary: This page proves Euler's formula using Maclaurin series expansions of e^(jθ), cos θ, and sin θ, establishing the polar form of a complex number z = re^(jθ). It summarizes the relationships between rectangular and polar forms, defines magnitude and angle of a complex number, and introduces the concept of the complex conjugate z* = a - jb = re^(-jθ), noting it is a mirror image about the real axis.
